# Provenance-First Principle

Date: 2026-03-01

## Principle

Every meaningful answer claim must map to retrieved evidence. Generation is constrained by the
evidence and must cite it; the system never invents uncited historical facts.

- Output format is claim then evidence.
- Each claim carries source ids / chunk ids.
- When evidence is weak, the system says so instead of guessing.

## Stability

This provenance-first principle is a fixed non-negotiable for the project and does not change across
iterations — it is the stable control against which evolving approaches are measured.
